title: agrostis vulgaris var. verticillata
intro: Agrostis vulgaris var. verticillata, also known as Red-top Bentgrass, is a type of grass native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its distinctive red or pinkish tint on its panicles. The medicinal properties of Red-top Bentgrass include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating gastrointestinal problems, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Red-top Bentgrass can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
